title: Payer/AddressProvided sessions not retried after reconnect
description: Trigger: payer receives Address (session moves to AddressProvided), then CLI disconnects and reconnects. resync_sessions has no arm for (Payer, AddressProvided). The address is stored in the DB so nothing is lost, but neither auto-payment nor a user-visible prompt fires after reconnect. Bad behavior: user must remember to type /confirm <id> with no prompt — will generate 'my payment got stuck after disconnect' reports. Fix: on reconnect, if send_fn is available and session is Payer/AddressProvided, re-trigger the auto-payment attempt (or print a visible reminder to /confirm).
close_reason: Fixed: resync_sessions now prints a visible reminder for each Payer/AddressProvided session ('Run /confirm <id> to send, or /cancel <id> to abort'). No peer message is sent (address already stored locally). Test: resync_payer_address_provided_sends_no_message verifies no channel message is emitted.
